title: Telangana Weather Alert: Heatwave up to 44°C, Thunderstorm & 50 km/h Winds Warning Issued for Multiple Districts - Munsif News 24x7
full_text:
A major Telangana weather alert has been issued, warning of severe heatwave conditions with temperatures reaching up to 44°C, along with thunderstorm, lightning, and strong winds up to 30–50 km/h in several districts.
Table of Contents
According to the Meteorological Department, areas including Adilabad, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Warangal, and Hyderabad are expected to face extreme weather in the coming days. The current status indicates an ongoing heatwave combined with thunderstorm risk, raising concerns for public safety.

The Telangana weather alert warns of heatwave conditions up to 44°C along with thunderstorm, lightning, and winds of 30–50 km/h in districts like Adilabad, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Warangal, and Hyderabad. title: 22 suspected heatstroke cases reported across Telangana - The Hindu
full_text:
With temperatures soaring across Telangana since March and intensifying sharply over the last two weeks of April, multiple districts have reported suspected cases of heatstroke or sunstroke, with Health department officials maintaining that no deaths have been recorded so far.
A telephonic survey of District Medical and Health Officers (DMHOs) across all 33 districts by The Hindu found that a total of 22 suspected cases have been recorded since the beginning of April. Among the districts reporting these cases, Hanumakonda and Mancherial reported five cases each, while Adilabad, Khammam, Karimnagar and Peddapalli logged three cases each.
Published - April 29, 2026 07:52 pm IST
